intel_records.context: PowerShell with encoded commands is a common execution technique for both APT and commodity malware. Frequently observed in initial access and lateral movement stages.
Spearphishing with malicious attachments remains the most common initial access vector. Office documents with macros, ISO/IMG containers, and HTML smuggling are current trends.
Newly registered domains, typosquatting domains, and domains with high entropy are strong indicators of phishing or C2 infrastructure.
Ransom notes are post-encryption artifacts. Specific note formats and ransom demand language can be used to attribute to specific ransomware families.
Public-facing exploit attempts may target known CVEs (n-day) or unknown vulnerabilities (zero-day). Pattern of probing followed by exploitation is typical.
